results.title: npx spikes-mcp starts MCP server
results.reason: In a clean first-run wrapper path, the wrapper attempted to download the darwin-arm64 binary and failed with HTTP 404, so the MCP server never started and no initialize response was returned.

evidence.step: Force clean first-run wrapper cache in isolated namespace temp directory and package local wrapper artifact
Run wrapper from clean path and send initialize JSON-RPC over stdio

output.stderr:
spikes-mcp: downloading binary for aarch64-apple-darwin...
spikes-mcp: Binary not found (HTTP 404). The release may not exist yet for this version.
Fallback: install the Spikes CLI manually:
  curl -fsSL https://spikes.sh/install.sh | sh
Then run: spikes mcp serve

evidence.step: Confirm no initialize response was emitted

frictions.description: Direct registry command 'npx --yes spikes-mcp' returned npm E404 (package not found) in this environment, so validation used a local packed wrapper tarball to test the wrapper runtime path itself.
frictions.resolution: Used npm pack on local packages/spikes-mcp and executed via npx --package <tarball> with isolated namespace cache/workspace.

summary: VAL-DISC-011 remains failing: first-run wrapper download for aarch64-apple-darwin still returns HTTP 404, preventing spikes mcp serve startup and initialize response from the normal wrapper path.
